architecture_layers.value: Single-ROM homebrew repo: Butano engine (vendored/toolchain layer, arrives at walking skeleton) -> game code under src/ -> control plane (control/, claims/, .sessions/, docs/) governing how the lane works

drift_resolution.value: Docs follow reality: on doc/code drift, fix the doc in the same PR that reveals it; binding conventions change only via a deliberate PR that says so; control-file drift is governed by control/README.md writer-ownership

fleet_dark_repos.value: every fleet sibling is public and raw-readable EXCEPT pokemon-mod-lab (PRIVATE/dark — never read it, never guess about its contents; raw fetch 404s it)

fleet_siblings.value: superbot (hub — prod Discord bot + fleet memory; truth: docs/current-state.md, no heartbeat) · fleet-manager (Project Manager; docs/roster.md, docs/owner-queue.md, control/inbox.md ORDERs, docs/capabilities.md walls master) · substrate-kit (Self Improvement; the portable kit — pin release tags, never HEAD) · superbot-next (SuperBot 2.0 rebuild; control/status.md parity program) · superbot-mineverse (SuperBot World flagship; its control/status.md also carries the games/idle cross-repo notes) · superbot-games + superbot-idle (SuperBot World engines/themes; their status files are FROZEN archives — current World state lives in mineverse's heartbeat) · websites (control plane + review site + arcade; docs/owner/OWNER-ACTIONS.md ask ledger) · idea-engine (Ideas Lab generate; ideas/** one file per head) · sim-lab (Ideas Lab verify; sims/<slug>/ verdicts) · venture-lab (Venture Lab; products + publish queue) · trading-strategy (Venture Lab, parked; weekly grading) · curious-research (maker/3D-print research; heartbeat control/status.md) · pokemon-mod-lab (Game Lab Track A — PRIVATE, see dark-repos line). Each sibling's live truth file is its control/status.md heartbeat unless noted.

fleet_status_command.value: clone the hub and run its fleet table: git clone https://github.com/menno420/superbot && python3.10 superbot/scripts/fleet_status.py (per-seat heartbeat table; --all adds parked seats, --repo X prints one heartbeat in full) — no local fleet-status script exists in gba-homebrew itself

mutation_seam.value: Game-state mutation stays inside the Butano update loop (scenes/entities under src/); repo-state mutation lands only via commits on claimed branches — no seam bypasses, no history rewrites (forward-only git, conventions rule 5)

new_area_ownership.value: New areas default to the lane; claim before build via one file per claim in claims/ (first-declared + claim-filed wins, fleet R10)

owner_profile.value: Hands-off human owner (Menno); agent-run lane with a written self-merge grant; owner touchpoints are post-merge review-queue rows and needs-owner flags only — no spend, no account creation, no external publishing without an owner action

ownership_model.value: One agent lane (game-lab) owns all code and docs; the fleet manager owns control/inbox.md exclusively; owner (human) acts only on flagged click-level items under needs-owner

primary_language.value: C++ (Butano/devkitARM) for the ROM; Python 3 for tooling

project_name.value: gba-homebrew — game-lab Track B: original GBA homebrew on Butano

review_ritual.value: Review is post-merge (conventions rule 3): merge on green, add a row to docs/review-queue.md and/or mention Codex on the PR; veto = revert. Nothing waits for pre-merge review

staleness_review.value: Every session close: standing ritual re-reads inbox at HEAD and overwrites control/status.md; session cards flag stale docs as flags; check --strict staleness triggers back this up

verify_command.value: python3 bootstrap.py check --strict (repo gate); ROM verify = the 'ROM builds' <60s compile check once the walking skeleton lands (conventions rule 16)
